title: Steroid resistance in nephrotic syndrome
abstract: A method for diagnosing steroid resistance in a subject having a glomerular disease such as nephrotic syndrome is described. The method includes the steps of providing a biological sample from the subject; determining the amount of at least one steroid resistance biomarker in the biological sample; and comparing the amount of the at least one steroid resistance biomarker to a control level of the at least one steroid resistance biomarker. The subject is diagnosed as having steroid resistance or a risk thereof if there is a difference in the amount of the at least one steroid resistance biomarker in the sample as compared to the control level. Determining whether the subject is steroid resistant can be used to guide treatment of the subject.
claims: Claims 1. A method treating a subject having nephrotic syndrome, comprising: (a) providing a biological sample from the subject; (b) determining the amount of at least one steroid resistance biomarker selected from Table 1 in the biological sample; and (c) comparing the amount of the at least one steroid resistance biomarker to a control level of the at least one steroid resistance biomarker; (d) treating the subject for nephrotic syndrome using a therapy other than steroid treatment if there is a difference in the amount of the at least one steroid resistance biomarker in the sample as compared to the control level. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the biological sample comprises blood, plasma, or serum. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the subject is human. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the subject is a pediatric human. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the steroid resistance biomarker is a polynucleotide. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the steroid resistance biomarker is a polypeptide. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the amount of the at least one steroid resistance biomarker comprises is determined using mass spectrometry (MS) analysis, immunoassay analysis, or both. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the amount of a plurality of steroid resistance biomarkers is determined. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the level of the steroid resistance biomarker is increased in subjects having steroid resistance. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the level of the steroid resistance biomarker is decreased in subjects having steroid resistance. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the steroid resistance biomarker is selected from adiponectin, EGF-containing fibulin-like extracellular matrix protein 1, inter- alpha-trypsin inhibitor heavy chain H4, hemopexin, antithrombin-III and vitamin D-binding protein. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein the therapy other than steroid treatment is selected from nutritional therapy, administration of a diuretic, and administration of a non-steroidal immunosuppressant. 13. A method for diagnosing steroid resistance in a subject having nephrotic syndrome, comprising: (a) providing a biological sample from the subject; (b) determining the amount of at least one steroid resistance biomarker selected from Table 1 in the biological sample; and (c) comparing the amount of the at least one steroid resistance biomarker to a control level of the at least one steroid resistance biomarker, wherein the subject is diagnosed as having steroid resistance or a risk thereof if there is a difference in the amount of the at least one steroid resistance biomarker in the sample as compared to the control level. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the biological sample comprises blood, plasma, or serum. 15. The method of claim 13, wherein the subject is a human. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the subject is a pediatric human. 17. The method of claim 13, wherein the steroid resistance biomarker is a polynucleotide. 18. The method of claim 13, wherein the steroid resistance biomarker is a polypeptide. 19. The method of claim 13, wherein the amount of the at least one steroid resistance biomarker comprises is determined using mass spectrometry (MS) analysis, immunoassay analysis, or both. 20. The method of claim 13, wherein the amount of a plurality of steroid resistance biomarkers is determined. 21. The method of claim 13, wherein the level of the steroid resistance biomarker is increased in subjects having steroid resistance. 22. The method of claim 13, wherein the level of the steroid resistance biomarker is decreased in subjects having steroid resistance. 23. The method of claim 13, wherein the steroid resistance biomarker is selected from adiponectin, EGF-containing fibulin-like extracellular matrix protein 1, inter- alpha-trypsin inhibitor heavy chain H4, hemopexin, antithrombin-III, and vitamin D-binding protein.
